name: wormhole
description: Evidence-gated planning orchestration for AI coding agents.

interface.longDescription: Wormhole provides a local MCP planning kernel with JSONL state, evidence records, open-question gates, Markdown plan artifacts, durable native context packs, context-pack budget review, reversible optimization records, optimized command execution, live sub-orchestrator control, adapter-free local orchestration runs, graph-first repo indexing, graph artifacts, repo watch sessions, git diff detection, repo activity recording, state maintenance coordination, project contract detection, structured diagnostics, impact-aware verification planning, safety scanning, deterministic semantic fallback search, safe LSP probes, LSP feedback replanning, one-shot project onboarding, repo blueprint and constraints artifacts, app-process product/roadmap/backlog artifacts, app-process run state/status/continuation evidence, native architecture maps, entrypoint flow discovery, blast-radius analysis, task-scoped project context packs, agent-facing routing, layered tool maps, structured tool catalog queries, next-tool recommendations, mission routes, prepared agent context, SQLite-backed durable repo indexes, durable semantic indexes, process-local LSP sessions, dependency security and live dependency risk reports, git lifecycle branch/commit/PR preparation, bounded conflict analysis, docs-sync gates, workspace graph analysis, action admission policy, managed patch transactions, optimization adapters, required Python runtime for graph and media analysis, native media ingestion, shell hook management, discovery-driven tool generation, learned orchestration policy gates, safe live policy feedback, orchestration policy baseline comparison, reasoning strategy research, printed-tool execution, native tool generation, validated writable tool scaffolds, external agent dispatch, shared agent workspace memory, durable resume continuation, executable external agent dispatch, Printing Press CLI registration, deterministic conductor scaffolds, durable brevity/minimality policy, model-profile traces, typed artifacts, and static workbench rendering. Codex should use the Wormhole loop for coding, review, onboarding, and planning: start a mission, start a round, start from workflow_start_feature, workflow_fix_bug, workflow_review_pr, or workflow_onboard_repo when the task matches those golden paths, run project_onboard when entering a repo, use app_process_compile or app_process_write_artifacts to produce product/roadmap/backlog process context, use app_process_status, app_process_accept_section, app_process_continue, and app_process_record_verification to resume the process loop from durable state, use resume_record for material decisions, blockers, verification, handoff, final response, and fresh-session recommendations, use resume_checkpoint before final output, heavy-context handoff, or a new chat, use resume_validate before claiming a session is safely resumable, use blueprint_compile_repo or blueprint_write_artifacts to produce repo-specific agent rules, use app_process_gate_check and blueprint_gate_check before completion claims, use project_intelligence_snapshot, tool_layer_map, tool_catalog_query, next_best_tool, mission_route, agent_context_prepare, and state_maintenance_run to choose a curated path and keep graph/context/evidence/workspace state synchronized before selecting lower-level tools, use architecture_map, entrypoint_flow_discover, blast_radius_analyze, and context_pack_generate to understand large repos natively, use ctx_pack_budget_review and ctx_pack_refresh when context packs overflow or stale records need eviction, use durable SQLite repo indexes and test_impact_analyze_v2 when changes are known, use dependency_security_report, dependency_risk_report, dependency_audit_live, git_lifecycle_status, git_branch_prepare, git_commit_prepare, git_pr_prepare, git_conflict_analyze, docs_sync_check, workspace_graph_analyze, and action_policy_review before risky implementation work, use git_branch_create and git_commit_create only after privileged admission review, use patch_checkpoint, patch_apply, patch_status, and patch_rollback for reversible edit transactions, use lsp_session_* when an installed language server is needed and lsp_feedback_replan when LSP diagnostics should revise mission scope, use optimization_adapter_* when external optimization adapters are configured, query the repo index before broad grep or raw file reads, gather and record evidence, record reusable context, register tasks when parallel work is useful, use agent_workspace_create, agent_workspace_write, agent_workspace_read, and agent_workspace_merge when concurrent workers need shared state, use optimized command runs and verification_run for large outputs or selected checks, use diagnostics_from_command and diagnostics_query for compiler/test findings, use semantic_index_build and semantic_search for deterministic local fallback search, use media ingestion for PDFs/images, use shell hook planning before shell profile edits, use discovery imports/crawls before generating API tools, use learned policy only after replay-gated activation and baseline comparison, use orchestration_policy_live_feedback for bounded live advice without self-activation, record reasoning traces when plan/critique/revision behavior matters, use local orchestration runs for deterministic task waves and outcomes, verify and run Printing Press CLIs before dispatch, validate and write bounded tool scaffolds when useful, execute configured CLI or HTTP agents when a real external run is required, request the gate before final claims, and only call emit_plan when the user explicitly asks for a plan, spec, design, or planning-only artifact.
defaultPrompt: Run this through Wormhole: mission_start, round_start, use workflow_start_feature/workflow_fix_bug/workflow_review_pr/workflow_onboard_repo when applicable, app_process_compile or app_process_write_artifacts for product/roadmap/backlog context, app_process_status/app_process_accept_section/app_process_continue/app_process_record_verification for durable app-process progress, blueprint_compile_repo or blueprint_write_artifacts for repo-specific agent rules, app_process_gate_check and blueprint_gate_check before completion claims, project_intelligence_snapshot, tool_layer_map, tool_exposure_profile, tool_catalog_query, next_best_tool, mission_route, agent_context_prepare, state_maintenance_run when state refresh is needed, durable_index_manifest_refresh/status and durable_repo_index_query for reusable large-repo indexes, workspace_graph_analyze for monorepos, repo_index_query before broad grep, git_lifecycle_status/git_commit_prepare/git_pr_prepare for release handoff, docs_sync_check and dependency_risk_report before final claims, record evidence, resolve questions, gate_request before final claims, and only call emit_plan when the user explicitly asks for a plan, spec, design, or planning-only artifact.

Use Wormhole orchestration for this feature: record context packs, refresh pack budgets when they overflow, inspect state_maintenance_status or use state_maintenance_retry after partial maintenance failures, create agent workspaces for shared concurrent state, register tasks, use tool_admission_review and action_policy_review before risky write/execute tools, use patch transactions for reversible edits, run focused verification, and use verified external agents or printed CLIs only when needed.
For implementation requests, make the scoped change, verify it, and summarize the result; for planning-only requests, emit a cited plan after the evidence gate opens.
